Description: A clamped floating-point number in the range [0, 1].
Role in Safety Contract: Provides a standardized, dimensionless measure of risk for a single physical variable.

Description: A composite struct containing one RiskCoord for each relevant plane (for example renergy, rcarbon, rbiodiversity).
Role in Safety Contract: Acts as a unified state representation, aggregating risk across all planes into a single object for processing.

Description: A struct containing non-negative floating-point weights (w_j) for each coordinate in the RiskVector.
Role in Safety Contract: Determines the relative importance of each risk plane in the calculation of the global stability metric.

Description: A scalar value calculated as V_t = sum_j w_j * r_j^2.
Role in Safety Contract: Serves as a global stability metric; its enforced non-increasing behavior ensures long-term safety and prevents runaway risk accumulation.

Description: A function that takes the previous residual, the proposed next residual, and the proposed next RiskVector as inputs.
Role in Safety Contract: Acts as the primary gatekeeper, rejecting any action that violates corridor bounds or Lyapunov-stability invariants.

Description: A set of deployment rules over a rolling window: K >= 0.90, E >= 0.90, R <= 0.13.
Role in Safety Contract: Defines the minimum acceptable K/E/R bands for a system to be considered production-ready based on governance policy and pilot data.
